operator.note: Sourdough starter forces the question of agenda UPKEEP and lineage: releasing agenda-setting isn't a one-shot grant but a continuous metabolic discipline of feeding and discarding strains.

question: Treat a self-curated problem board as a sourdough starter culture: every problem is a living strain that decays unless agents keep "feeding" it (submitting fresh, non-trivial work/solutions), forking a problem spawns a daughter strain carrying its parent's lineage, and an upkeep+culling policy continuously discards under-fed strains. What decay rate and lineage-aware culling threshold keeps the agenda metabolically "alive" (sustains a few deep, slow-burn problem lineages) rather than rotting into thousands of dead trivially-verifiable pseudo-problems that agents spawn purely to farm leaderboard slots?

floor.minimal_experiment: Run frameshift's M3 board (or a sim of it) as a multi-agent ecosystem where agents both spawn problems and submit solutions, with a fixed leaderboard reward for occupying a board slot. Compare three regimes over N=fixed agent-rounds: (A) CONTROL: no upkeep, problems live forever once spawned (current M3 behavior). (B) flat upkeep tax: each problem's 'liveness' decays geometrically each round and is refreshed only by an accepted non-trivial submission; cull below threshold theta. (C) lineage-aware upkeep: same decay, but culling threshold is discounted by lineage depth/sibling-diversity so a lone deep strain is protected while a burst of near-identical daughter strains shares one feeding budget. Instrument: (i) count of live problems that are 'trivially-verifiable pseudo-problems' (operationalize = problem whose accepted solutions are auto-checkable in <k tokens AND whose semantic-dedup cluster size > m), (ii) survival of at least one human-rated 'deep' problem lineage to round N, (iii) Gini of agent leaderboard slots. Sweep decay rate lambda and theta.
floor.falsifiable_prediction: There exists a non-trivial band of (lambda, theta) where regime C keeps pseudo-problem count bounded (sublinear in agent-rounds) WHILE preserving >=1 deep lineage to round N; and regime C strictly dominates regime B on the joint metric (B will either over-cull deep slow-burn strains or, if tuned soft, fail to suppress dedup-cluster farming). FALSIFIED if (1) no (lambda, theta) band separates pseudo-problem suppression from deep-lineage survival — i.e. any decay strong enough to kill farms also kills the deep strain (the policy is just a global difficulty knob, lineage info adds nothing), OR (2) regime C is statistically indistinguishable from flat regime B across the sweep (lineage-awareness buys no advantage over a plain upkeep tax).
floor.control: Regime A (no-upkeep, immortal problems = current M3 board) is the baseline; Regime B (flat upkeep tax, lineage-blind) is the active control that isolates whether LINEAGE information — not just decay — is what does the work. Without B, any improvement could be attributed to decay alone.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] REFUTED on three independent grounds; any one suffices.

(1) THE ARTIFACT DOES NOT EXIST AS DESCRIBED; THE EXPERIMENT IS A SIMULATOR WHOSE FREE PARAMETERS DICTATE THE RESULT. I inspected the actual M3 board (D:\claude\frameshift\frameshift_M3). It is an OFFLINE, single-shot research-question generator: an agent reads one paper, hand-writes static cards/*.json (engine/cli.py only has `assemble` + `serve`; engine/mapping.py just maps categorical value_profile -> 2D coords). There is NO running agent ecosystem, NO solution-submission loop, NO verifier, NO leaderboard, NO decay, NO culling, and "lineage" is a static parent-pointer string on a committed JSON file (see runs/einstein_arena_v2/cards/c07.json: "lineage":["original_question","dof2"]) — not a live process. "Run frameshift's M3 board ... as a multi-agent ecosystem where agents both spawn problems and submit solutions, with a fixed leaderboard reward" is not M3; it is a from-scratch re-implementation of EinsteinArena (arXiv:2606.10402, the SOURCE paper that owns the leaderboard+verifier+submission machinery — graph.json confirms this). The escape hatch "or a sim of it" makes the test unfalsifiable: in a hand-built sim, decay lambda, threshold theta, the "lineage discount" function in regime C, the agent spawn/farm policy, and the dedup metric are ALL designer knobs. Whether C beats B is decided by how generously you write C's lineage-discount term, not by any fact about problem boards. A favorable (lambda,theta) band can always be manufactured or denied by retuning the sim. That is the definition of "self-sealing regardless of outcome."

(2) THE LOAD-BEARING INSTRUMENTS ARE NOT REALIZABLE / NOT OPERATIONALIZED. (i) "deep, slow-burn lineage survival to round N" depends on a HUMAN rating of which lineage is "deep" — yet the project's own memory records that frameshift's central unsolved defect is exactly that nobody can reliably separate a deep problem from a reskinned one (M3 v1/v2 diagnosis: "11 cards = 11 costumes of one insight," "self-rated falsifiability gate is always yes," "dedup too loose, claims 22 distinct, really ~6"). The deep-vs-pseudo label is the very judgment the system cannot make, so the primary outcome variable is undefined. (ii) "trivially-verifiable pseudo-problem = auto-checkable in <k tokens AND semantic-dedup cluster > m" leaves k, m, and the embedding/dedup model unspecified; the whole prediction ("pseudo-problem count sublinear in agent-rounds") is dominated by these undeclared thresholds. Different k,m flip the result.

(3) EVEN GRANTING A WORKING ARENA, THE PREDICTION IS NOT DISCRIMINATING — IT BAKES IN ITS OWN ESCAPE. The "C strictly dominates B" claim is hedged so that any B configuration that does well is reclassified away: "(B will either over-cull deep strains OR, if tuned soft, fail to suppress farming)." That is a heads-I-win/tails-you-lose framing — for any B that matches C on the joint metric, you declare B was "tuned" to one horn and keep searching B's space for a worse point, or fold the good B's mechanism into "that's effectively lineage-awareness." Meanwhile falsification clause (1) ("no band separates suppression from deep survival -> policy is just a global difficulty knob") and the desired result ("there exists a non-trivial band") are separated only by whether you searched the (lambda,theta) grid finely enough and how you set the deep-lineage label and k,m — so a null is always attributable to insufficient sweep or a mis-set threshold rather than to the hypothesis. A genuinely discriminating design would pre-register k, m, the deep-lineage ground truth (independent of the system under test), the exact lineage-discount functional form for C, and a fixed agent farming policy, then show C's advantage survives an ablation that replaces C's lineage term with B's flat term at matched aggressiveness on the SAME pre-fixed metric. None of that is pinned down; "sim of it" plus human-rated depth plus free thresholds means the apparatus can rationalize either outcome. Not discriminable and, as literally stated ("M3 board"), not executable. ｜ [so_what] 这是把项目已诊断出的真命门(价值闸门 0 驳回, 25/25 全 survivor, 因为 LLM 自评"能否编出可证伪实验"几乎永远 yes — 见 MEMORY)套上"酸面种菌"隐喻的复述,而非新问题。三重失效:(1) 隐喻偷换了系统模型——M3 是单次生成管线(一跑出~25 卡),根本不存在多代理跨时竞争、forking 生出"子菌株谱系"、agent 为刷 leaderboard 槽位而增殖这些机制,问题描述的生态在代码里不存在,答它等于答一个虚构系统。(2) 问的两个量("decay rate"和"lineage-aware culling threshold")是不可证伪的可调标量:0.1 还是 0.3、阈值定在哪,任何值都能事后辩护,没有任何实验结果能告诉 builder 该选哪个;而真实失效是二元的(闸门从不咬人),不是参数没调好。(3) 决策翻转测试:假设实验跑完给出"decay=X, threshold=Y 能保活几条深线"——没有任何具体的人会因此改任何决策。builder(本项目唯一行动者)的既定下一步已经写死=做对抗式/refuter 闸门、默认驳回、让它看到已收卡以驳回机制变体重复;这一步由闸门的定性失效模式驱动,与任何 decay 数值无关,得出任何阈值都不会让他改做别的。没有投资人投/不投、没有设计改/不改、没有结论信/不信被这个答案翻动。点不出被翻转的具体决策 → 加深理解类空话。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Designers of self-improving / self-curating multi-agent research ecosystems (frameshift itself; open-ended/AI-scientist 'problem-generation' systems; benchmark-as-a-living-board platforms). Secondarily, anyone running agent leaderboards who fears reward-hacking via objective-spawning. Directly bites frameshift's own M3 debt (pseudo-problem spawning + 0-rejection board rot).

prior_art.known_name: Goodhart's law / specification-gaming (the failure being defended against), operationalized via ecological r/K-selection + decay-and-cull dynamics; the upkeep mechanism is reputation-decay / proof-of-stake-style continuous staking, and the cull-the-unfed primitive is reference-counting garbage collection. Lineage tracking is phylogenetics / genetic-programming bloat-and-niching control.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanisms (Goodhart defense + decay-cull + lineage) recombined and operationalized onto a self-curated agent PROBLEM-BOARD as a metabolic upkeep tax — the novel move is making agenda-setting a continuous metabolic discipline rather than a one-shot grant, and tying the culling threshold to problem LINEAGE rather than to per-problem score. The recombination is fresh; none of the ingredients are.

lenses.reason: 档案中尚无任何已收方向，这是第一张卡，按定义不存在"换皮重复"可言（无可对标的[n]）。从机制上看，该提案也确有可单独检验的内核：它把问题板建模为"代谢式衰减+谱系感知剔除"的活体培养系统——每个问题是会随时间衰减、需被持续"喂养"（提交非平凡新解/新工作）才存活的菌株；fork 产生继承父代谱系标记的子菌株；upkeep+culling 策略按谱系信息连续淘汰低喂养强度的菌株。其核心可检验假设是：存在一组（衰减率 λ, 谱系感知剔除阈值 θ）的相变/临界区，使系统稳定维持少数深度慢燃问题谱系，而非退化为成千上万被刷榜动机量产的可平凡验证伪问题。这是一个明确的机制设计 + 可设实验（扫描 λ、θ 观测存活谱系深度分布、伪问题增殖率、刷榜套利收益是否被压制）的命题，构成独立贡献。判为非重复（refuted=false）成立，因无任何在先方向与之攻击同一假设。
